title: Chrysanthemum rupestre
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum rupestre is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, such as the arrangement of its flowers and leaves. The genus Chrysanthemum is a group of flowering plants that are native to East Asia and are often cultivated for their ornamental value. The species Chrysanthemum rupestre is a type of wild chrysanthemum that grows in rocky areas. Chrysanthemum rupestre is also sometimes classified as a synonym of the species Leucanthemopsis rupestris.

intro: Chrysanthemum rupestre, also known as Korean mountain chrysanthemum, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum rupestre include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its pharmacological activities.
